heading: Certain uses of excess foreign currencies
label: 5495 Certain uses of excess foreign currencies

children.heading: Authority to use
children.label: a Authority to use
children.text: During fiscal year 1990, the Administrator of the Agency for International Development may use, for the purposes described in subsection (b), such sums of foreign currencies described in subsection (c) as the Administrator may determine, subject to subsection (f).

children.heading: Purposes for which currency may be used
children.label: b Purposes for which currency may be used

children.text: for the same purposes for which assistance may be provided under part I of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( and following; relating to economic assistance), and22 U.S.C. 2151

for the support of any institution providing education for a significant number of United States nationals (who may include members of the United States Armed Forces or the Foreign Service or dependents of such members).

children.heading: Currencies which may be used
children.label: c Currencies which may be used
children.text: The foreign currencies which may be used under this section are United States-owned excess foreign currencies that are in excess of amounts necessary for satisfaction of preexisting commitments to use such currencies for other purposes specified by law.

children.heading: Where currencies may be used
children.label: d Where currencies may be used
children.text: Foreign currencies may be used under this section in the country where such currencies are held or in other foreign countries.

children.heading: Nonapplicability of other provisions of law
children.label: e Nonapplicability of other provisions of law
children.text: Foreign currencies may be used under this section notwithstanding or any other provision of law.section 1306 of title 31

children.heading: Requirement for appropriations action
children.label: f Requirement for appropriations action
children.text: The authority of this section may be exercised only to such extent or in such amount as may be provided in advance in an appropriation Act.
